tasks.title: Update pine wood to have darker texture
tasks.notes: Item sprite for pine wood is still the same as oak and is not visually consistent with the tree currently in the game. Update item sprite to match source.

tasks.title: Insufficient resource highlight [context offers]
tasks.notes: When crafting or trading, if the player has insufficient resources to craft or trade, the text will display red, indicating the insufficiency.

tasks.title: Player Hurt VFX & SFX
tasks.notes: Player hurt sound effects and vfx flash needed to make combat feel more alive and realistic. Feels very flat at the minute.

tasks.title: Design Relic/Discovery Event
tasks.notes: Relics in Evergreen will reveal important parts of the storyline. First relic needs to be thoroughly planned out and designed to land, HARD.

tasks.title: Tool swing rhythm reward. Spam gets punished.
tasks.notes: See if a rhythm based tool use can reward the player with one less hit to fell a tree or mine a stone. Spamming gets punished

tasks.title: Title Screen
tasks.notes: Create a title screen to greet the player when they launch the game.

tasks.title: Shadow Silhouette Walk Animation
tasks.notes: General polish to death shadow silhouette. Walk animation, maybe revise general display, feels quite small and as though he doesn't fit. Add a first time sequence "Gabh mo leithscéal!" - irish for "Excuse me" little easter egg.

tasks.title: Torch low fuel flicker
tasks.notes: When torch fuel is <= 20%, begins to flicker.

tasks.title: Player sprite displays currently held tool.
tasks.notes: Update the player tool use animations as well as walk/idle sprites to show currently selected tool in their hand,

tasks.title: Torch "Dark" and "Deep" radius pass.
tasks.notes: Currently, torch is basically making the dark and deep zones of the forest "daytime", ruining the whole vibe of the darkness, mystery and the unknown.

tasks.title: Update the tree shadow
tasks.notes: Current tree shadow is residual from the old oak tree sprites. Needs to update to more accurately respect the triangular shape of the pine trees.

tasks.title: Dark/Deep Resource Glow Flicker
tasks.notes: Subtle flicker of "glow" effect emitting from glowing resources found in the deep and dark areas of the forest.

tasks.title: Forest Darkness Zone Darkness
tasks.notes: At present, the canvas modulate only activates when the player  enters the darkness zone. Player can see the locations of enemies, resources, relics, etc when not even in the zone. Darkness must show at all times, revise CanvasModulate system, if it is best fit for our system.

tasks.title: Dark and Deep forest area tileset
tasks.notes: Dark and Deep forest zones must be visually distinct from the other zones. As well as the Ancient Grove, will be different again.

tasks.title: Complete one forest design
tasks.notes: Update one forest layout to use all of the new assets and work visually.

tasks.title: Tile Farming System Abstraction
tasks.notes: Player farming logic is tightly coupled to World class. Implement a shared interface (e.g. ITileFarmingOwner) so all maps (World, Forest, future biomes) support tilling, overlays, and farmland logic without type checks.

tasks.title: Update Deep and Dark areas to use new tiles.
tasks.notes: Place new dark and deep tiles in deep and dark areas of the forest to improve the feel of the zones and make them seem more environmentally consistent.

tasks.title: Show Context of Tool to be upgraded at workbench
tasks.notes: When showing "upgrade tool" show icon of tool to be upgraded (axe, pickaxe). Clearer context required.

tasks.title: UI Pass of Relic Lore Breadcrumb
tasks.notes: Fix issue with signpost to better show message hinting to the relic system.

tasks.title: Slow Player Relic Progression
tasks.notes: Broken bridge. High HP rocks, etc. Slow players relic progression, force them to discover higher tool tiers. Collecting materials, cooking system, etc.

bugs.title: Player dropping items from wrong slot wrong quantity
bugs.notes: When the player selects a slot and presses the drop key "Q" the quantity of items will drop from the leftmost slot of that item in the inventory in the quantity in the current slot.

bugs.title: Campfire and grindstone incorrectly showing context offers.
bugs.notes: When the player has not yet crafted the workbench, both the campfire are showing crafting offers, that cannot yet be executed... Also the idea of showing the context offer and having to craft it at the workbench... maybe once the player has the workbench repaired/unlocked, they now can see the offers at the workbench and unlock them at their respective stations.

bugs.title: Farmland tiles drawing above player.
bugs.notes: When player tills soil to plant crops, the tilled farmland tiles draw above the player. Planting functionality still works just the draw z-indexing.

bugs.title: Paths can be tilled
bugs.notes:
Player is able to till paths as they are an overlay layer to grass. Hoe detects grass tile which is under the path.
# Likely fix, update path tile to better integrate with grass, when integrated on the same TileMapLayer.

bugs.title: WorldTime begins when game runs, not just when game loads.
bugs.notes: Game time is still ticking when sitting on title screen

sessions.title: Y-Sorting and Scene Loading Refactor
sessions.body: Added trader cycle offer sound effect. Began y-sorting integration. One way or another this caused the scene loading system to absolutely shit itself. Had to rip out the redundant, half-used (unintentionally) SceneManager system. Main is now the solo scene loader and game manager. Y-sorting is roughed in, trees are shifting alpha when player walks behind but sorting still needs tweaking. Cut down to 2 forest variants — others were bloaty and would need serious refactoring. Left for a future session.

sessions.title: Y-Sort Pushed to All WorldObjects
sessions.body:
Y-sorting polish completed.
New GroundYSort contains all world props, enemies, trees, etc. Allows for proper y-sorting and clean project organisation.
Y-Sorting implemented for all world objects, all scenes set up woth consistent tree structure.
Key scripts have been cleaned up, general housekeeping of class organisation and method organisation.
Added "Death Silhouette" polish. When the player dies, to add to the lore of the game. As the player is waking up from death at bed spawn. You can see a dark sihouette walking toward the boundary of the forest. The identity is hidden for the demo. The player cannot catch the silhouette.
Tweaked the timing and delay settings of the cutscene, feels good now.
Z-index housekeeping. Text/thoughtbubbles was rendering under boundary tiles, etc. Now redners on +5 z-index.

sessions.title: Wandering Trader Polish
sessions.body:
Added Wandering Trader spawn and flee sfx. Combination of a leaf flurry and a soft rising whoosh.
Trader spawn animation created in aseprite. Using as flee placeholder. Trader visit feels much more polished now.
Trader flee animation placeholder replaced with smoke esque screen effect.
Glowshroom item and forageable resource added to game. Player can now harvest glowshrooms.
Ancient oak added. Static idle animation. Now a source of resin in thew world.
Added glow to glowshrooms - the name hints at it. Resin pool at the top of the "ancient oak" stump has glow too.

sessions.title: Crafting Offer Cycling
sessions.body:
[Bug] Turns out "considerable bug with crops" was just that the definition for the turnip was overwritten at some point and wasn't spotted. Added back now.
Crafting offer priority system is very buggy.
New context offer priority system deisgned. Using save architecture from the trader offer cycling being reused here.
Cycling logic has been added. Player can cycle the current crafting option using "F".
Player can no longer attack after death and while respawning. Death, Passout, Cutscene system refactored to block all input and output.
Evergreen HQ, cross-platform game dev tracking and productivity app V1 stable and live.

sessions.title: Context Offer Insufficience Highlight
sessions.body:
If the player attempts to craft or interact with the trader, the context offer system will flag in a red highlight what resource the player does not have enough of, making it clearer to the player what they are deficient in.

---

Context offers now show the item icon with the quantity. If the player has the required quantity, the text and icon appear white, if player has insufficient resources, icon shows dimmed red with red quantity, indicating insufficiency to the player.

---

If multiple resources are required to craft/trade, items are now ordered from right to left showing where you're short if you are to the leftmost and where you have sufficient to the right.

sessions.title: Context Offer System Unification
sessions.body:
All workbench, campfire, trader, grindstone, have all been updated to use a shared contract for the context offer system. Each were implemented at different times and resulted in having the same system written in slightly different ways.
Unlocking/crafting the cooking station now occurs at the campfire.
Unlocking/crafting the grindstone happens at the grindstone.

---

Player hurt sound effects and vfx, overall "juice" polish added. Now the player plays a random sound effect from a bank of 6 when hurt from any source.

---

Player can now drop items from inventory correctly. Multiple stacks will drop the correct number from the correct stack, rather than the leftmost.

---

Designed and implemented third forest design. Took multiple iterations. Different style and feel. Began implementation of the reli/ anxiety grove idea.

sessions.title: Devlog 13
sessions.body:
Recorded, edited, created thumbnail and published Devlog 13.
Fixed bug where temporary thought bubbles where getting overwritten and disappearing quicker than intended.

sessions.title: Forest Darkness and Torch Pass
sessions.body:
Reworked the forest darkness system to be player-driven instead of zone-based.
Torch now scales with darkness depth, and only consumes fuel when it’s actually needed.

---

Successfully created an auto-tiling grass tileset. Tested in editor and much quicker than manual placement.

sessions.title: Ground TileSet Autotiling
sessions.body:
Created 47 tile auto-tiling sheets for paths. Works really well.
Converted the 3x3 grid tilesets for dirt and stone to 47 tile auto-tiling sets too.
Created new TileMapLayer child of TileMap in forest for tiles that we don't want to border with other tiles, just lay on top. Path tiles are example of this.

sessions.title: Forest Boundary Art
sessions.body:
Created "dynamic" sprites for the forest boundary.
Low Shrub, Mid Shrub and a dumbed down version of the pine tree will form the world boundary, not a tilemap.
This allows for a much more natural world edge feel, not something that has been crafted by hand... even if it has.

sessions.title: Testing Placement of New Boundary Tiles
sessions.body:
Tested placement of new boundary tiles on world edges. Natural generated feel at boundary now.

---

Reset PC to become lean ARM game dev command centre. Godot was crashing consistently, clean install was the play.

sessions.title: Farmland Update
sessions.body:
Created a new auto-tiling tileset for the farmland tiles.
New rigged new tileset up to hoe tool. Farmland tiles also use autotiling when adjacent tiles are tilled.

sessions.title: Farmland tiling and bug fixing
sessions.body:
Updated the "worked" soil sprites to match the new farmland tiles. Overlay tiles are borderless and intended to cover stones, worked soil = greater yield.
[Bug] tilling was creating a "cross" shape. Tilling the target tile but also tilling the adjacent tiles too in one smack.

sessions.title: Devlog 14
sessions.body:
Recorded, edited, thumbnailed and published devlog 14.
Fixed location of bed respawn point as well as the silhouette spawn and exit points.

sessions.title: Shadow Polish
sessions.body: Updated the shadow for the pine tree to better reflect its shape. Needs a final pre-demo polish but looks vastly more accurate now.

sessions.title: Forest Design
sessions.body:
Completed the forest design for variant 1.
Added a "backup" invisible collision boundary. Player can no longer enter Narnia when they get a lucky break between two trees.
Modified locations of spawnpoints and exits in both world and forest variant 1 to make it feel more intentional when entering and leaving an area. World needs slight extension of exit corridor (make it better match entrance to forest.

sessions.title: Map Entry/Exit
sessions.body:
Map entry and exit areas designed to be more seamless to move from one area to another.
Collision boundaries updated too to reflect new changes.
Slight redesign to clearing map.

sessions.title: Deep and Dark Tileset
sessions.body:
Created ground tileset to be used in deep and dark areas of the forest.
Updated tilemaps to have terrains as different sets so that when placing different tiles on same TileMapLayer, tiles don't use boundary tiles on embedded tiles.

---

As well as resolving boundary tiles adjoining issue, path tilling issue no longer happening.

sessions.title: Deep and Dark Autotiliing
sessions.body: Set up autotiling for deep/dark areas. After placing some test tiles, boundary is nor working with transparent bs. Need to create updated "grass-bound" tilemap for the deep/dark areas

sessions.title: Dark Deep Tileset
sessions.body:
Updated *again* the deep/dark tileset to look better. Best version yet but still not taking the cake yet.
Fixed a bug where when tilling the farmland on first interact was just becoming a grass tile intersection rather than a farmland asset tile. Corrected the Terrain Index and Terrain Set Indexes to pull from the right tilemap for tilling interactions.

---

Also, corrected bug where the z-index of the tilled and worked soil tiles were drawing on top of the player. Now player draws on top always again.

---

Fixed bug where player with torch equipped in the clearing was still illuminating in the clearing. Just had to port over the logic from the forest and wire it up in the clearing.

sessions.title: Devlog 15
sessions.body: Recorded, edited and published devlog 15.

sessions.title: Torch Flicker
sessions.body: Natural and realistic flicker added to use of the torch. Slight variance in scale and energy when in normal use and then a more volatile flicker when fuel is low, indicating to the player more clearly that the fuel is low.

sessions.title: Deep Forest Pulse
sessions.body:
Added a slow pulse to the deep forest forageables to subtly draw attention to them. Currently affects glowshrooms and ancient oak trees.
Re-configured git properly to properly point to the GitHub repo to continue backing up Evergreen to the repo.

sessions.title: Farming System Abstraction
sessions.body:
Created ITileFarmingOwner interface.
Moved farming responsibility out of Player and into the map itself.
Player now checks the map for whether a tile is tillable, whether it is farmland, and whether it has a crop, etc.
Removed direct World and Forest type checks and removed farming logic from specific scenes.

---

Updated Pine Log Icon to match pine tree colour scheme.

---

Created the first ancient relic. Structurally in, scene created, sprites created. First easter egg for the game, the first relic will reference the "Holy Stone of Clonrichert" - reference to Father Ted. Spit and polish required; SFX, VFX polish, but conceptually in.

sessions.title: Relic Polish
sessions.body:
Added a "blast" visual effect from the red stone in the relic when discovered, coupled with the whoosh sound effect.
Also added a ambient hum when the player enters a certain radius of the relic. Volume rises as the player edges closer to the relic too.
First implementation was using code based leash detection but proved inefficient, replaced with an Area2D entry exit system.

---

Final polish, hum fades out when left the area rather than a kind of hard cut. More polished feel overall.

sessions.title: Save System and Title Screen
sessions.body:
Implemented a comprehensive save to disk system,. Game now  saves player progress between sessions.
To complement this, a stylised title screen has been created too to allow the player to load from a save file or clear the save and start afresh.

sessions.title: Title Screen and Screen Text Polish
sessions.body:
Added a low-to-no UI title screen to evergreen. This is to support the save/load system. Rule for the game is that there is no saving mid-day just once day is completed (either by sleeping, passing out or dying and time progressing to the next day). Created a first pass logo for Evergreen too. Title screen needs stylisation and polish but functionally does its job.
[Bug] All text from thought bubbles and context offers was being  overlayed by CanvasModulate that is creating darkness zones. Updated so that text is on CanvasLayer now so darkness doesn't affect it.

sessions.title: Devlog 16
sessions.body: Recorded, edited, created thumbnail and published Devlog 16.

sessions.title: Title Screen Polish
sessions.body:
Added Keycap Letters to load and restart prompt text.
Fade in for logo and prompt text.
Restart no longer autoloads into game.

sessions.title: Weapon Loadout
sessions.body:
Weapon Loadout system added. Player had a sword, but unbeknownst to myself, it was still the roughed in sword, simply to demo the function that has been in since day 1. I wanted to add some progression to the sword so that the player could upgrade it so that it would deal some more damage. Removed residual logic from the ToolLoadout system.

---

Added upgrade logic to the workbench to allow the player to upgrade sword. 1 -> 2 dmg.
Also configured so that the weapon upgrades are written to the save file also.

sessions.title: Pause Menu
sessions.body:
Created an aesthetic, minimalist pause screen.
Gameplay and time pause in the background.
[Bug] Game time not pausing when game launches and waits on title screen.
Resolved, WorldTime now remains paused until E is pressed to load save (new or old) and play game.

sessions.title: Relic Discovery Event & Lore
sessions.body:
Relic discovery event now drops a parchment with some lore on it.
Created a "parchment scroll" art asset, proves too finicky. Going to redesign to flat paper in next lock-in block.
Lore can be written to data files, infinitely scalable.
Player now gets buffed with +1 stamina after relic discovered.

sessions.title: Lore Overlay Issues
sessions.body:
Issue after issue after issue... Lore parchment margins, not lining up.
[Bug] Relic discovery event was triggering a save to file.
Expanded relic asset to cover area closer to the aspect of the screen.
Still needs a polish and rework to ensure text will display correctly for different message lengths etc.

sessions.title: Lore Discovery Polish
sessions.body:
Finally got the relic lore discovery event displaying correctly!
Added an upgrade preview for the workbench, making it much cleaner and obvious as to what you are upgrading, reducing potential mistakes.

sessions.title: Forest Variant 2
sessions.body: Created a new, custom forest layout. 2/9 complete, to mimic procedural generation in the demo.

sessions.title: Forest Variant 3
sessions.body:
Created a new, custom forest layout. 3/9 complete, to mimic procedural generation in the demo.
Added lore data #2, "The Bell at Dusk". Dropping some lore about the Wandering Trader. Resource created.
[To-Do] need to update the relic system to display correct relic chronologically, regardless of the current days forest. (i.e. same relic will show on every map until discovered, then new one loaded, each day until discovered.

sessions.title: Relic System Refactor and Lore
sessions.body:
Refactored the relic discovery system to display relics and their associated storylines in the correct order. Rather than having specific relics per scene, that would prove tricky when having the cycling forest variations.
Completed writing all 5 of the relic lore discovery storylines and implemented it into the relic discovery system.

sessions.title: Relic Blessing Rewards
sessions.body:
Updated the relic blessing rewards to better reflect the story. Also added the ability to grant blessings of +HP and +StaminaRegen as well as +Max Stamina.

---

Applied distinct colours to each of the different relics, so player knows that it is a different relict that they are discovering each time.
[Bug] Signal Disconnect throwing loads of errors after 5th relic discovered. Improved signal wiring hygiene in Relic.cs

sessions.title: Signpost Breadcrumb
sessions.body: Added a signpost to the clearing to give the player a contextual hint to the existence of the relic system.

sessions.title: Signpost Breadcrumb
sessions.body: Created visual art asset for the breadcrumb signpost UI overlay clean up.

sessions.title: Devlog 18
sessions.body: Recorded, editeed and uploaded Devlog 18.

sessions.title: Breadcrumb Thoughtbubble
sessions.body:
Decided to veto the UI overlay for the signpost and just bolt on a "Hold" for a few seconds on the thoughtbubble system. Also justified the thought bubbles and context offers to the left and added text wrapping and a max line length.
Began designing the progressing gating for the 5 relic beats.
Created a sprite for the fallen pine tree gating access to relic 2 for the demo.
Roughed in persistence and player awareness of the fallen tree gate. Not yet functional.

sessions.title: Fallen Tree Gate
sessions.body:
Created deeper sound effects for chopping fallen tree.
Implemented upgrade tool loadout upgrade blocker, with prompts/hints for player.
Fallen tree now gates rapid progression to relic 2.
[Bug] Fixed "ghost" boar sound effect when loading new forest variant.

snippets.title: Devlog Script Template
snippets.code:
**EVERGREEN DEVLOG GENERATOR — MASTER PROMPT**

You are helping me turn raw weekly dev notes into a YouTube devlog.

IMPORTANT — TONE & STYLE:

* Natural, grounded, conversational
* Sounds like I’m talking to myself while working
* No YouTuber voice, no hype, no corporate language
* No Gen-Z slang, no forced humour
* Slightly rough is GOOD
* Honest, self-aware, accountable
* Calm confidence — not overexcited
* Avoid poetic/try-hard phrasing

WHAT I DON’T WANT:

* “Hey guys welcome back…”
* “This week was insane…”
* “In today’s devlog…”
* Over-explaining or sounding scripted
* Listing features like patch notes

WHAT I DO WANT:

* Real thoughts
* Admitting when progress was slow or messy
* Emphasising FEEL over features when relevant
* Subtle observations like:
  “doesn’t look like much, but it feels completely different”

---

## INPUT (I will paste below this):

* Weekly dev journal notes (messy is fine)
* Optional extra context

---

## OUTPUT STRUCTURE (you must follow this exactly):

### 1. SCRIPT (2–3 min YouTube devlog)

* Start with a HOOK (not greeting, not title repeat)
* Flow like natural speech
* Group ideas (don’t list daily logs)
* Keep it tight, no fluff
* Keep sentences speakable out loud
* End with a clean, grounded outro

---

### 2. TITLE OPTIONS (3)

* Short, curiosity-driven
* Not clickbait, but strong contrast or intrigue
* Avoid generic devlog titles

---

### 3. THUMBNAIL TEXT (3 options)

* 2–4 words max
* Simple, punchy, emotional or curiosity-driven
* Must NOT repeat title wording

---

### 4. DESCRIPTION

Structure:

* First line = hook (linked to title)
* Then short paragraph explaining what happened
* Mention systems/art/changes naturally
* No waffle

---

### 5. TAGS (comma separated)

* Relevant to indie dev, Godot, pixel art, etc.

---

## EXTRA RULES:

* If the week is low output → LEAN INTO IT (don’t hide it)
* If the progress is visual → emphasise FEEL and VIBE
* If something “clicked” → build narrative around that
* Avoid repeating the same phrasing too often
* Keep everything sounding like ONE person thinking out loud

---

## MY DEV NOTES:

gdd.title: Concept and Vision
gdd.body: Evergreen is a pixel art survival game in Godot 4.5 using C#.

gdd.title: Core Mechanics
gdd.body:
Stamina-based combat (5 stamina, 2 per attack)
Sleep-based health regeneration
Food and hunger system

gdd.title: Enemies
gdd.body:
Boar — doubles as food source
Thornweaver — area-control thorns

gdd.title: Demo Scope
gdd.body: Define what is and is not in the demo here...

lastDevlog:

I wanted to add some progression to the sword so that the player could upgrade it so that it would deal some more damage. Removed residual logic from the ToolLoadout system.
